overview: Esper Control in Legacy aims to stabilize the board through efficient interaction and card advantage, eventually closing out games with resilient finishers. By leveraging Ponder for perfect sequencing and Cabal Therapy for massive resource generation, the deck outlasts aggressive strategies and punishes combo decks. The primary path to victory revolves around protecting and deploying Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to secure an unblockable, damage-dealing presence.
win_conditions: Deploying Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to deal unblockable damage each turn
Outdrawing opponents through Cabal Therapy and Ponder to exhaust their resources
Stabilizing against early aggression with efficient removal and counterspells before transitioning to a late-game finisher

keep_criteria: Hands with two or more lands and early interaction
Hands containing Ponder or Cabal Therapy to enable card selection
Hands with a clear path to stabilize turn three or four
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, Ponder, and at least one early interaction (Cabal Therapy, Path to Exile, or a counterspell).
Keep any hand with Ponder + 2 lands.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with zero interaction or removal
Hands with three or more lands without early plays
Hands lacking a clear game plan against the expected matchup
Mulligan pure land draws, hands with multiple Ponders but no lands, or hands with only late-game threats and no early answers.

early_game: Play lands and hold up mana for potential counterspells or removal
Use Ponder to find answers to the opponent's early threats
Prioritize surviving the first few turns over developing your own board
Turn 1: Play a land, Ponder if possible to set up draws.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast Cabal Therapy against combo/midrange, or hold for a threat.
Sequence lands carefully to ensure blue mana availability for counterspells and card draw.
Prioritize stabilizing plays over aggressive ones.
mid_game: Cast Cabal Therapy to draw multiple cards and find specific answers
Begin deploying resilient creatures or planeswalkers if the board is clear
Maintain a hand of interaction to disrupt opponent's key turns
Preserve Ponders for when you need a specific answer or threat.
Don't overcommit mana to Ponder if you can't protect it.
Manage the graveyard with Cabal Therapy or Surgical Extraction against Tron/Sneak and Show.
Keep hand size above 3 when possible.
Hold back on committing Ajani or Nacatl Pariah until you've established a baseline of interaction.
Use Cryptic Command or Jace to clear blockers or protect your board.
late_game: Cast Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and protect it with counterspells or removal
Use excess card advantage to outvalue opponents in long games
Cycle through your hand to find the exact answer needed to close the game
Ajani for lifelink and board presence, Nacatl Pariah for aggressive pressure, and Jace for card advantage.
Win by out-resourcing and closing with a board of threats or a planeswalker.
Go all-in when you have card advantage secured, the opponent has no hard removal, and you can protect your finisher with counterspells or lifegain.
Commit when you can swing and still have an answer to their removal.

key_cards.name: Ajani, Nacatl Pariah
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and resilient finisher
key_cards.notes: Cast on your turn when you have mana open to protect it, and use its +1 ability to generate lifegain and protect it from removal. Prioritize keeping it alive through combat and spells.

key_cards.name: Cabal Therapy
key_cards.role: Massive card advantage and resource generation
key_cards.notes: Use it to draw enough cards to find specific answers or to flood your hand with interaction. Sequence it after Ponder to maximize the quality of cards drawn.

key_cards.name: Ponder
key_cards.notes: Cast it at the end of your turn to look at the top five cards, ensuring you draw the exact answer or land needed for the next turn. Always shuffle the top card back if it is not useful.

key_cards.name: Lightning Bolt
key_cards.notes: Hold up one red mana to clear aggressive threats or reduce opponent's life total. Use it reactively to preserve card advantage and stabilize the board.

sequencing_tips: Always cast Ponder at the end of your turn to optimize your opening hand for the next turn
Sequence Cabal Therapy after drawing into it to immediately find answers to the opponent's current threats
Hold up white and blue mana early to counter or remove aggressive creatures before they become unmanageable
Protect Ajani, Nacatl Pariah by keeping counterspells or removal in hand until the opponent commits to attacking it
Cabal Therapy is versatile but can be dead against control
Ajani's lifelink is crucial against aggressive decks.
Cryptic Command is a win-more card in some matchups but essential in others.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early interaction and life total preservation. Use Ponder to find removal and counterspells, then stabilize before deploying Ajani, Nacatl Pariah.
watch_for: Pressure that forces you to play suboptimally

matchups.advice: Disrupt their key combo pieces with counterspells and removal. Use Cabal Therapy to draw multiple answers and find the exact card needed to stop their win condition.
watch_for: Card draw spells that enable their combo
Hidden win conditions in their hand
Attempts to go wide or cheat creatures into play

matchups.advice: Race to deploy Ajani, Nacatl Pariah while maintaining a hand of interaction. Use Cabal Therapy to outdraw them and find answers to their sweepers and counterspells.
watch_for: Board sweepers that clear your finishers
Counterspells held up for your key plays
Attrition games where card advantage decides the match

matchups.advice: Use Ponder/Brainstorm to dig for answers or threats that can't be countered. Cast threats in response to counterspells, or use Cryptic Command to bounce their counter source. Don't overextend into known counter suites.
Early Cabal Therapy to disrupt mana dorks/Tron. Surgical Extraction to remove key permanents. Path to Exile to trade efficiently. Don't let them stabilize; pressure early.

Win the card advantage war. Prioritize Ponders/Brainstorms and Jace. Use Cryptic Command to bounce their threats or protect yours. Cabal Therapy to disrupt their hand. Win by out-drawing and out-resolving.

common_threats: Aggressive creature decks that overwhelm early interaction
Combo decks that bypass traditional removal
Board sweepers that clear resilient finishers
Watch for Tron's signets/mana rocks, Sneak and Show's graveyard setup, Delver's flip threshold, and Ocelot Pride's mana acceleration.
Note if they're holding up blue mana (counterspell) or black mana (Therapy).
